g.text: reach: every namespace in the cluster
The same three edge classes as the gateway, and the same ClusterRole/Role split
ClusterRoleBinding applies in every namespace, so anything that must be namespace-limited (Leases, Secrets)
has to ship as a separate namespaced Role.
The operator does not hold cluster-wide Secret access.
sidecar model, credentials stay with the gateway in agentry-system and are never copied to user namespaces.
Kubernetes escalation prevention forbids creating a Role that grants permissions the
creator does not itself hold, and the per-channel Roles grant user-namespace Secret reads the operator
deliberately lacks as a standing permission.
satisfies that check when creating or updating the Role.
is separately required for the RoleBinding half: Kubernetes permits creating a RoleBinding referencing a
Role only if the creator either holds everything the Role grants, or holds bind on it. One verb does not cover both.
This is a deliberate trade, and the operator ServiceAccount should be audited as privileged.
satisfies the escalation check without escalate is granting the operator standing Secret read across all user
namespaces, which is strictly worse: it turns a create-time capability into an always-on read surface.
